name: pressure_fed_lox_kerosene_system_8b50b801
description: Design a pressure-fed rocket engine feed system using LOX (liquid oxygen) and kerosene as propellants, with nitrogen (GN2) as the pressurant gas. The system comprises three tanks (GN2, LOX, kerosene), pressurization lines from GN2 to each propellant tank, and feed lines from each propellant tank to the engine. The GN2 tank provides pressurization to maintain propellant tank pressures, forcing propellants through the feed lines to the engine. FIXED: component architecture (GN2 tank, LOX tank, kerosene tank, engine, GN2-to-LOX pressurization line, GN2-to-kerosene pressurization line, LOX feed line, kerosene feed line). TUNABLE: tank initial pressures, volumes, temperatures; line diameters and lengths; simulation duration.

notes: Use the pressure_fed_lox_kerosene seed as the starting point
Ensure GN2 pressurizes both LOX and kerosene tanks
Both propellants must flow to the engine
Preserve the Engine node and the two feed Series connections into it.
Tune feed CdA values before changing topology.
If physical warnings mention a component, fix that component first.

checks.description: Simulation completes without crash or invalid configuration
No nonphysical or degenerate warnings during simulation
LOX flows through lox_feed_line to engine
Kerosene flows through kerosene_feed_line to engine
GN2 flows to pressurize LOX tank
GN2 flows to pressurize kerosene tank
LOX tank has valid pressure samples
Kerosene tank has valid pressure samples
GN2 tank has valid pressure samples
LOX feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
Kerosene feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
